top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 81, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
101ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Eroica South Africa's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
At Eroica South Africa, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Eroica South Africa's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 81 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Eroica South Africa. Train it until it feels boring.
8,200ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 81 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
8,200ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 81 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Low-cadence force work for 8,200ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 81 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.heat: April in Montagu, Western Cape, South Africa can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Eroica South Africa. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Eroica South Africa’s 81 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Eroica South Africa requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 81 miles of mixed terrain in Montagu, Western Cape, South Africa.
